steps.title: Spot the key signal
steps.content: ”May make it seem” establishes a reasonable perception that can nonetheless be disputed — the word “may” signals the perception isn't definitive, setting up the “but” clause that will prove it can be challenged.

steps.title: Apply the signal to choices
steps.content:
B identifies exactly what the underlined portion does: present a sensible perception of Shurin's anthology that the following text shows can be disputed for good reason.
A misplaces focus on specific authors; C wrongly generalizes a limited observation; D shifts to genre definition disputes.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
